news.headline: Analyst expects Microsoft AI revenue to surpass $10B in 2025, maintains Overweight rating with lowered price target of $470.
news.summary: Analysts at Piper Sandler have provided insights into the upcoming first-quarter results announcement by Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT), set for the end of October. Analyst Brent Bracelin, from Piper Sandler, reiterated an Overweight rating on Microsoft shares but adjusted the price target downwards from $485 to $470. Bracelin highlighted potential challenges in the company's first-quarter guidance and results, suggesting they may be "sloppier than normal" due to changes in key performance indicator (KPI) models. The introduction of new KPI metrics is expected to bring significant changes. For the complete story, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Old Dominion Freight Line downgraded by analyst with lowered price target of $210 from $214. Industrial demand remains sluggish.
news.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (NASDAQ:ODFL) saw a slight dip in its shares on Friday following a downgrade by BMO Capital Markets analyst Fadi Chamoun. The analyst revised the company's rating to Market Perform from Outperform and decreased the price target from $214 to $210. Chamoun highlighted a significant decrease in consensus/BMOe F2025 earnings expectations by 13%/15% due to ongoing muted demand in the freight markets and delays in the anticipated recovery timeline. The analyst cautions that further negative revisions may be on the horizon as the forecasted recovery for freight markets prolongs. Details on industrial demand and more can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk's Alhemo drug gets recommendation in Europe for hemophilia treatment; first once-daily subcutaneous prophylactic treatment for age 12+ with hemophilia A or B with inhibitors.
news.summary: Novo Nordisk A/S announced on Friday that the European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use has recommended approval of Alhemo (concizumab) as the first daily subcutaneous prophylactic treatment for hemophilia patients aged 12 and above with inhibitors. Hemophilia is a rare bleeding disorder that hinders blood clot formation necessary to stop bleeding. Alhemo, an anti-TFPI monoclonal antibody, has shown effectiveness in preventing bleeding episodes regardless of dosing frequency. If approved by the European Commission, it will provide the first daily subcutaneous prophylactic treatment option for these patients.

news.headline: FDA approves Roche's companion diagnostic to identify patients with gastric cancer for targeted treatment with VYLOY, fulfilling unmet need.
news.summary: The recently developed VENTANA CLDN18 (43-14A) RxDx Assay addresses a critical medical gap by allowing medical professionals to pinpoint individuals with gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) cancer who could potentially gain from a specific treatment. CLDN18.2 stands out as a rising biomarker in gastric and GEJ cancer cases, offering significant promise for targeted therapeutic interventions.

news.headline: PAI Partners raises bid for Sanofi's consumer health unit as French govt hints at possible regulatory block.
news.summary: Last week, Sanofi SA disclosed negotiations with Clayton Dubilier & Rice (CD&R) regarding potentially selling a 50% controlling stake in Opella, its consumer healthcare business. This move comes after Sanofi's announcement in October 2023 to spin off its consumer healthcare business as part of its strategic update to boost investment in drug development and reduce costs. The French government has raised concerns about the potential sale to CD&R, hinting at blocking it due to worries about employment and national healthcare interests. France's stipulations during the sale process may impact the outcome.

news.headline: Astellas' VYLOY approved by FDA for treating advanced gastric & GEJ cancer. First CLDN18.2-targeted treatment in US.
news.summary: Astellas Pharma Inc. announced that VYLOY has been approved in the U.S. as the initial and sole CLDN18.2-targeted therapy for adults with advanced gastric and gastroesophageal junction cancer displaying CLDN18.2 positivity. This milestone was revealed in Tokyo on October 18, 2024. Astellas Pharma Inc. (TSE: 4503), led by President and CEO Naoki Okamura, spearheaded this development.

news.headline: European Medicines Agency panel supports Novartis' Kisqali approval in early-breast cancer; for adjuvant treatment of HR+/HER2- EBC at high risk; positive opinion adopted.
news.summary: Novartis AG revealed on Friday that the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use has given a positive opinion for Kisqali (ribociclib). The CHMP suggested approving marketing authorization for Kisqali for the adjuvant treatment of adults with hormone receptor-positive/human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-negative early breast cancer at high risk of disease recurrence, encompassing individuals with node-negative disease. This decision signifies a significant step forward in potentially providing a new treatment option for individuals with this form of breast cancer.

news.headline: California American Water approved to acquire Mesa del Sol Water System in Corral de Tierra, adding 15 new water connections to its existing base of 40,000.
news.summary: In Pacific Grove, California, the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) has officially sanctioned California American Water’s acquisition of the Mesa del Sol water system situated in the Corral de Tierra region of unincorporated Monterey County. With this acquisition, around 15 new water connections will be included in the company’s existing customer base, which currently comprises roughly 40,000 connections across the Monterey Peninsula and various other areas within the County. This move marks California American Water’s expansion and strengthened presence in the local water system sector.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in the last decade. The government has expressed optimism about the situation and plans to continue implementing policies to sustain this economic progress.

news.headline: FY2024 unaudited financial report released for the 12 months ending June 30, and a meeting notice issued.
Partnership between Rohirrim and SMA, Inc. aims to revolutionize proposal development using AI. Oct 18, 2024 Irvine.
news.summary: In a historic collaboration announced on October 18, 2024, Rohirrim and SMA, Inc., The Program Lifecycle Company, have joined forces to revolutionize the approach to proposals. Through the introduction of RohanRFP's integration with The SMA Way, which combines their extensive experience in proposal development, organizations will benefit from a new and innovative way of handling proposals.

news.headline: Dominica launches online immigration forms for travelers today to streamline entry process. Effort aims to simplify travel experience.
news.summary: Today, in Roseau, Dominica, a new initiative has been launched to enhance the travel process for visitors. Online immigration forms will now be available for embarkation and disembarkation, starting on October 18, 2024. The goal of this program is to streamline the entry process and improve the overall experience for travelers. This change is expected to significantly reduce the time and effort required for individuals entering and leaving the country.

news.headline: Edifier announces MR3 2.0 Monitor Speaker System, ideal for home studios, video editing. Release date: Oct. 18, 2024.
news.summary: Introducing the MR3 2.0 monitor speaker system from Edifier International, a renowned audio electronics designer. This system is perfect for home studios, video editing, and everyday listening, ensuring honest reproduction of sound. The speaker system holds Hi-Res audio certification and is designed to provide high-quality sound output. Announced on October 18, 2024, in Richmond, BC, this product aims to cater to the needs of audio enthusiasts with its superior performance.

news.headline: CVRx introduces new CPT codes for Barostim to treat heart failure symptoms, approved by AMA.
news.summary: On October 18, 2024, CVRx, Inc. (NASDAQ: CVRX), a medical device company, revealed that the American Medical Association (AMA) CPT® Editorial Panel has approved new Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Category I codes for Barostim, a therapy to address heart failure symptoms. This move signifies a significant milestone for CVRx as they introduce innovative treatment options. The acceptance of these codes showcases the growing recognition and adoption of Barostim therapy within the medical community.
